why_use_sql.label: Why Use SQL?
why_use_sql.summary: Common use cases and importance of SQL.

sketch1.title: identify scenarios where SQL is used

sketch2.title: explain the importance of SQL in data management

sketch3.title: How to practice Why Use SQL?

concept_overview.title: What is Why Use SQL??
concept_overview.bodyMarkdown:
Why Use SQL? focuses on this idea: Common use cases and importance of SQL.

A good starting point is to say the requirement in plain language before writing SQL.

### Learning goals
- identify scenarios where SQL is used
- explain the importance of SQL in data management

goal_one.title: identify scenarios where SQL is used
goal_one.bodyMarkdown:
identify scenarios where SQL is used.

When practicing Why Use SQL?, start with a focused example and verify the result carefully.

goal_two.title: explain the importance of SQL in data management
goal_two.bodyMarkdown:
explain the importance of SQL in data management.

Use the topic idea to explain what the query is doing in plain English.

practice_note.title: How to practice Why Use SQL?
practice_note.bodyMarkdown:
Read the requirement, predict the result, run the query, and verify whether the output really answers the question.

That habit is especially important for Why Use SQL?.

module0_why_use_sql_practice_sql.title: Practice: Why Use SQL?
module0_why_use_sql_practice_sql.prompt:
The `products` table already exists.

Write a query that shows the `name` and `price` columns for rows whose `price` is greater than `50`.

Then run it.

help.concept: Common use cases and importance of SQL.

module0_why_use_sql_practice_sql.hint: Use WHERE with a comparison operator.
module0_why_use_sql_practice_sql.starterCode:
-- Filter rows by price
SELECT name, price
FROM products
WHERE price > 50

module0_why_use_sql_core_concept.title: Core idea: Why Use SQL?
module0_why_use_sql_core_concept.prompt: What is the best description of Why Use SQL??

help.concept: Common use cases and importance of SQL.
help.hint_1: Look for the option that matches the main concept directly.
help.hint_2: Ignore answers that describe unrelated SQL behavior.

module0_why_use_sql_core_concept.hint: Match the answer to the topic summary.

options.a: It keeps only the rows that match a condition.

module0_why_use_sql_true_statements.title: True statements about Why Use SQL?
module0_why_use_sql_true_statements.prompt: Select all true statements about Why Use SQL?.

help.concept: Common use cases and importance of SQL.
help.hint_1: Pick statements that align with the topic summary and objectives.
help.hint_2: Reject statements that clearly describe unrelated SQL ideas.

module0_why_use_sql_true_statements.hint: Use the summary and objectives as your guide.

options.a: Filtering is often done with WHERE.
options.b: Comparison operators help define the row condition.
options.c: Filtering always changes the stored table data.
options.d: AND and OR can change which rows stay in the output.
options.e: Filtering is unrelated to business questions.

module0_why_use_sql_workflow_order.title: Workflow for Why Use SQL?
module0_why_use_sql_workflow_order.prompt: Reorder the workflow ideas for Why Use SQL?.

help.concept: Identify which rows should stay, write the condition, then verify the result.
help.hint_1: Think about what should happen first.
help.hint_2: Then think about what should happen last.

tokens.t1: decide which rows should stay in the result
tokens.t2: write the condition in the WHERE clause
tokens.t3: run the query and verify the remaining rows

module0_why_use_sql_keyword_fill.title: Filtering keyword
module0_why_use_sql_keyword_fill.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best SQL keyword.

help.concept: Common use cases and importance of SQL.
help.hint_1: Think about which SQL term best completes the sentence.
help.hint_2: Use the topic meaning to eliminate unrelated options.

module0_why_use_sql_keyword_fill.hint: Match the sentence to the right SQL term.
module0_why_use_sql_keyword_fill.template: The clause commonly used to filter rows is ____.
